title: Paraixeris sonchifolia
taxonomy: Paraixeris sonchifolia is classified as a member of the dandelion tribe within the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Paraixeris and the species P. sonchifolia. The genus Paraixeris is a group of flowering plants that are native to East Asia. These plants are annual or biennial herbs, often with a single stem and simple leaves. They are typically found in disturbed areas, such as roadsides and fields.

intro: Paraixeris sonchifolia, also known as the Korean dandelion or Korean chicory, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to East Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and fever, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Paraixeris sonchifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Korean medicine, Paraixeris sonchifolia is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies for various health conditions.
